core_mechanism: exact differentiation of a finite-order trigonometric polynomial (finite Fourier series in a parameter) via a closed finite weighted sum of the function evaluated at a fixed set of discretely shifted argument values, rather than an infinitesimal finite-difference limit
brief_reason: The abstract derives n-th order 'parameter-shift rules' that compute exact gradients of a parameter-dependent probability from finitely many shifted evaluations and benchmarks them against finite differences, which is the signature of exactly differentiating a finite-Fourier-series-in-the-parameter response by a finite linear combination of shifted samples.
